class.name: Trail3D
class.brief_description: A node to draw lines behind moving objects.
class.description: The [Trail3D] node draws a flat line in space, with an optional billboard mode. [b]Note:[/b] This node is designed for visual effects. For solid lines, pipes, and tubes, use a [CSGPolygon3D] node in [constant CSGPolygon3D.MODE_PATH] mode.

method.name: get_current_length
method.description: Returns the current length of the trail.

members.member: The color of the trail. The color is readable in the shader through the vertex color. [b]Note:[/b] Colors with values above [code]1.0[/code] (overbright) are not supported.
The color gradient over the trail length. The color is readable in the shader through the vertex color. [b]Note:[/b] Colors with values above [code]1.0[/code] (overbright) are not supported.

If [code]true[/code], the trail will generate new points from its head. [b]Note:[/b] Only one active trail is supported. Setting this property to [code]true[/code] will cause the existing trail mesh to be cleared.
The lifetime of the trail when [member limit_mode] is [constant LIMIT_MODE_LIFETIME].
Whether to limit the trail by lifetime or length.
The material mode for the trail. Use [constant Line3D.MATERIAL_MODE_CUSTOM] to supply a custom material.
The maximum length of the trail when [member limit_mode] is [constant LIMIT_MODE_MAX_LENGTH].
Alignment of the trail. Possible options are [constant Line3D.MESH_ALIGNMENT_LOCAL] for a 3D line, and [constant Line3D.MESH_ALIGNMENT_BILLBOARD] for a billboarded line.
Minimum required distance before adding a new section.
If [code]true[/code], pins the UVs to their positions in space.
How to tile the UVs across the length of the trail.
UV multiplier across the length of the trail.
Width across the length of the trail.

constants.constant: Limit the trail length in time.
Limit the trail length in size.
Represents the size of the [enum LimitMode] enum.
